title: Linguistics Deep III: Emergence of Meaning & Deep Compositionality

symbol_grounding_gap.grounding_step: EML-∞ (the gap Searle's Chinese Room exposes)

symbol_grounding.key_insight: Symbol grounding = EML-∞: no EML-finite map from syntax to semantics

figurative_vs_literal.live_metaphor: EML-∞ (novel cross-domain blend = not EML-finite computable)
figurative_vs_literal.irony: EML-∞ (meaning = opposite of literal: EML-∞ inversion)

metaphor_and_blending.key_insight: Conceptual blending is EML-2 (geometric mean dim); emergence = EML-∞

language_origin_gap.origin_transition: EML-∞ (the crossing itself is irreversible and non-analytic)

language_evolution.key_insight: Language evolution = EML-1; origin transition (signal→symbol) = EML-∞

eml_depth_summary.EML-2: MI grounding, pragmatic inference (Bayesian), metaphor productivity
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: No natural linguistic example at this level
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Symbol grounding (syntax→semantics), live metaphor, language origin transition

key_theorem: The EML Semantic Emergence Theorem: All formal linguistic operations (syntax, compositionality, pragmatic inference) are EML-0 to EML-2. The emergence of meaning from symbols — the symbol grounding problem — is EML-∞: there is no EML-finite algorithm that takes a formal symbol system and outputs the semantic content it represents. This is Searle's Chinese Room in EML language.
rabbit_hole_log: Symbol grounding = EML-∞: Searle's insight = EML asymmetry theorem
Pragmatic inference = Bayesian = EML-2 (same as free energy, PMI)
Conceptual blend dim = geometric mean = EML-2; emergence = EML-∞
Language origin: signal (EML-1) → symbol (EML-∞) = EML-∞ transition
No EML-3 in linguistics: oscillations/waves don't naturally appear in semantics

connections.S136_linguistics_v2: Extends S136 with symbol grounding + metaphor + origin
connections.S131_cognition_v2: Symbol grounding ↔ hard problem: both = EML-∞ explanatory gaps
connections.S140_grand_synthesis_8: Language emergence = major transition = EML-∞ (per Horizon Theorem)
